task_completion.description: Create the 'Breaking News Alert' reusable block
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a WordPress reusable block (or synced pattern) named 'Breaking News Alert' containing the text 'BREAKING NEWS: This is a developing story. Check back for updates.' in the block editor or patterns list.

Look for a WordPress reusable block (or synced pattern) named 'Newsletter Signup' containing the text 'Enjoy our reporting? Subscribe to our daily newsletter to stay informed on the stories that matter.' in the block editor or patterns list.

Look for a WordPress reusable block (or synced pattern) named 'Editorial Disclaimer' containing the text 'Editor's Note: The views expressed in this article are those of the author and do not necessarily reflect the editorial position of this publication.' in the block editor or patterns list.

task_completion.description: Create a post with the correct title and a paragraph about the budget
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a post in the WordPress editor or front-end with the exact title 'City Council Approves New Budget Plan' and a paragraph of text discussing a budget.

task_completion.description: Insert at least two of the reusable blocks into the post
task_completion.visual_evidence: Within the 'City Council Approves New Budget Plan' post, look for the exact text of at least two of the three created reusable blocks, indicating they have been added to the post content.
Look for a 'Post published' confirmation message, the post status showing as 'Published' in the editor sidebar, or the post being viewed on the live front-end of the website.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual WordPress web interface
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a web browser navigating the WordPress admin dashboard and block editor, not a text editor or mock interface.

integrity.description: Agent did not use terminal commands to bypass the GUI
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal windows showing wp-cli commands, curl requests, or database modifications to create the blocks or post.

Look for evidence of the agent using the WordPress 'Create pattern' / 'Add to reusable blocks' feature, or navigating the Patterns management screen, rather than just typing the text directly into the final post.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected reusable blocks to be created are: 1. 'Breaking News Alert' (Keywords: 'breaking news', 'developing story'), 2. 'Newsletter Signup' (Keywords: 'subscribe', 'newsletter'), 3. 'Editorial Disclaimer' (Keywords: 'editor's note', 'editorial position'). The expected post title is exactly 'City Council Approves New Budget Plan'.
